     The Numbers Business Report

Collateral Beauty (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $31,016,021Details
International Box Office $57,500,000
Worldwide Box Office $88,516,021
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,197,653
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,093,617
Total $3,291,270
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When a successful New York advertising executive suffers a great tragedy he retreats from life. While his concerned friends try desperately to reconnect with him, he seeks answers from the universe by writing letters to Love, Time and Death. But it’s not until his notes bring unexpected personal responses that he begins to understand how these constants interlock in a life fully lived, and how even the deepest loss can reveal moments of meaning and beauty.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,102,085 (22.9% of total gross)
Legs:4.37 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:35.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$36,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,528 opening theaters/3,528 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $42,555,403

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 16th, 2016 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: December 16th, 2016 (Wide) (Mexico)
December 16th, 2016 (Wide) (Netherlands)
December 16th, 2016 (Wide), released as Косвена красота (Bulgaria)
December 16th, 2016 (Wide) (Slovakia)
December 16th, 2016 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
Video Release: February 28th, 2017 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic elements and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2446 (Cert #50724), 10/12/2016)
Running Time: 96 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Geographical setting: New York City
Plot point: Cancer, Death of a Son or Daughter, Depression, Dysfunctional Family, Infidelity, Romance, Terminal Illness, Unconventional Pregnancies
Social setting: Advertising and Marketing
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: PalmStar Media, Likely Story, New Line Cinema, Village Roadshow Productions, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment, Anonymous Content, Overbrook Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

When a successful New York advertising executive suffers a great tragedy he retreats from life. While his concerned friends try desperately to reconnect with him, he seeks answers from the universe by writing letters to Love, Time and Death. But it’s not until his notes bring unexpected personal responses that he begins to understand how these constants interlock in a life fully lived, and how even the deepest loss can reveal moments of meaning and beauty.
